figures.caption: Table 2. Comparison performance of slide-level gene expression estimation on TCGA-BRCA, KIRC, and LUAD. MIL: multiple instance learning, MIR: multiple instance regression, PCC: Pearson correlation coefficient, and SCC: spearman correlation coefficient

Figure 6. The parameter distribution for cell-type prototype on BRCA before and after training. Each scatter plot corresponds to a specific prototype, with individual points representing genes. The horizontal axis denotes parameter values before training, while the vertical axis represents values after training.
Table 3. Comparison of methods across CSCC, Her2st, and STNet datasets. Each (+digit or -digit) shows the difference from the baseline
Figure 4. Average weight for each subtype of BRCA slides. We visualized the estimated weights for the cancer-related prototypes.

Figure 3. Overview of our method. Gene expression is estimated based on cell-type prototype T̄ and weight w(x (n) i ). The prototype supports the integration of covariance of gene expression among cells.
